idea: How to price your freelance projects without underselling
hook: Stop guessing your rates — price on value, not hours

slides.headline: Price on value, not on hours
slides.body: Five shifts that raise your rates without scaring clients away.

slides.headline: Anchor to outcomes
slides.body: Lead with the result you deliver — revenue, time saved, risk removed — before you ever mention a number.

slides.headline: Quote a range, then narrow
slides.body: Open with a range tied to scope. It frames the conversation and filters out mismatched budgets early.

slides.headline: Charge for the decision, not the deliverable
slides.body: A logo is cheap. The judgment behind it is not. Price the expertise clients cannot easily replace.

slides.headline: Add a premium tier on purpose
slides.body: A higher option makes your middle package feel like the sensible choice — and some clients pick the top.

slides.headline: Before you send the quote
items: You tied price to scope, not hours
You offered two or three clear tiers
You removed one point of friction

slides.headline: Raise your rate on the next one
slides.body: Pick one shift and apply it to your very next proposal.
slides.kicker: Save this for your next quote

caption:
Most freelancers underprice because they sell hours instead of outcomes.

These five shifts reframe the conversation around the value you create — so your rate reflects the result, not the clock.

Which one will you try on your next proposal?
